title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 11 February 2025.

items.title: **Marvel Rivals** rolls back controversial mid-season rank reset hours after announcement
bullets: Last night, it was announced in 10 days that players would have a rank reset part-way through the current season of Marvel Rivals.
After extremely negative feedback, Netease has rolled back the decision mere hours after the announcement.
Instead of a rank reset, players will have to to play 10 matches to redeem the rewards for their rank following the mid-season update.

items.title: **Valve ** reminds devs they can't use mobile-like in-game paid advertising on Steam
bullets: Developers cannot use paid advertising as a business model for their game, such as requiring players to engage with ads before playing, or locking gameplay behind ads

items.title: Ghost-hunting sim **Phasmophobia ** hits 2m console sales, and 22m sales overall
**Square Enix** financial earnings for the nine months ending December 31, 2024
bullets: Net sales: ¥248.5 billion ($1.6 billion, down 3.5% year-on-year)
Digital entertainment net sales: ¥160.3 billion ($1.05 billion, down 10.7% year-on-year)
